title: Bootcamp Scheduler — Constraint Solver Practical
description: Build a real production constraint solver. But first, build the tools you'll use to build it.

exercises.title: Build a Spec Skill
exercises.description: Write a /spec command in CLAUDE.md that interviews you and produces a high-quality technical specification — with acceptance criteria, test cases, edge cases, and engineering notes. You'll use this for every challenge that follows.

steps.title: Understand what a good spec contains
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read challenges/01-spec-skill.md and explain the difference between a weak spec and a strong one'

steps.title: Look at the example
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read specs/example.md and explain why each section is there'

steps.title: Write the skill
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to help you create .claude/commands/spec.md. You decide what it should do — Claude helps you express it precisely.

steps.title: Test your skill
steps.instruction: Run /spec and describe the availability parser (from challenge 03). Review specs/current.md — is it good enough to implement from?

exercises.title: Build a Build Skill
exercises.description: Write a /build command that takes your spec and produces working, tested code. It should plan before coding, respect existing code, run tests, and fix failures.

steps.title: Understand the pattern
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read challenges/02-build-skill.md. What are the key properties of a good build skill?'

steps.title: Look at an example command
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read .claude/commands/next.md and explain how a Claude command file is structured'

steps.title: Write the skill
steps.instruction: Ask Claude to help you write .claude/commands/build.md. Think about the 5 phases: pre-flight, plan, implement, verify, report.

exercises.title: Availability Parser
exercises.description: Use your new tools to build the foundation: parse an Excel spreadsheet into a structured availability dictionary.

exercises.title: Find Valid Time Slots
exercises.description: Identify which day pairs can host a bootcamp — consecutive days (Mon-Tue or Thu-Fri) with at least 2 trainers free on both.

exercises.title: Greedy Scheduler
exercises.description: Assign trainer pairs to slots using a greedy approach.

exercises.title: Experience Rules
exercises.description: Enforce trainer seniority — every bootcamp needs an experienced trainer, trainees must be paired with one.

exercises.title: CP-SAT Optimal Solver
exercises.description: Replace the greedy approach with Google OR-Tools CP-SAT to find the globally optimal schedule.

steps.title: Read the challenge
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read challenges/07-constraint-solver.md and explain why greedy is provably sub-optimal on this dataset'

exercises.title: Caps and Limits
exercises.description: Add per-trainer weekly day caps and per-week total bootcamp limits, read from the intermediate.xlsx spreadsheet.

steps.title: Read the challenge
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read challenges/08-caps.md and show me what intermediate.xlsx looks like'

exercises.title: Location Awareness
exercises.description: Schedule against location demand. Some locations (Paris) require French-speaking trainers.

exercises.title: Full Production System
exercises.description: Add bank holidays, trainer weightings, and travel penalties. Then run all 46 tests.

steps.title: Read the challenge
steps.instruction: Ask Claude: 'Read challenges/10-full-system.md and explain the three features'

steps.title: Feature A: Bank holidays
steps.instruction: /spec bank holiday blocking, then /build it, then python3 -m pytest tests/test_08_full.py::test_good_friday_blocks_london_trainer -v
